perturbation.original_text: TERM: The rights granted hereunder shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2006 (the "Term"); provided, however, that with respect to each Property, including Robots, Kingdom of Heaven, Mr. and Mrs. Smith, In Her Shoes, Idiocracy (Oww My Balls) and Ice Age II, all right and licenses granted herein will continue in full force and effect until March 31, 2008.
perturbation.changed_text: TERM: The rights granted hereunder shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2006 (the "Term"); provided, however, that with respect to each Property, including Robots, Kingdom of Heaven, Mr. and Mrs. Smith, In Her Shoes and Idiocracy (Oww My Balls), all right and licenses granted herein will continue in full force and effect until March 31, 2008.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Ice Age II' from the list of properties with extended terms, the contract creates uncertainty regarding the license expiration date for 'Ice Age II'. While the initial expiration date is December 31, 2006, the subsequent clause extending the term to March 31, 2008, now explicitly excludes 'Ice Age II'. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because Section 1 could be interpreted to mean that rights for Ice Age II now expire at the initial date, while other references to 'each property' might imply it should still be included in the extended term. Later mentions of 'all properties' without specifying exceptions would become contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXTENSION OF TERM: The first paragraph of Section 4 of the Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "TERM: The rights granted hereunder shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2006 (the "Term"); provided, however, that with respect to each Property, including Robots, Kingdom of Heaven, Mr. and Mrs. Smith, In Her Shoes and Idiocracy (Oww My Balls), all right and licenses granted herein will continue in full force and effect until March 31, 2008."
perturbation.justification: The original text included 'Ice Age II' in the list of properties with extended terms, whereas the changed text removes it. The contradicted text (which is also the changed text in this case) reflects this removal. This creates a direct contradiction because earlier versions of the agreement might have specified a uniform expiration date for *all* properties, or referred to 'each Property' assuming 'Ice Age II' was included. Now, 'Ice Age II' is explicitly excluded from the extended term, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes about its license expiration date, given the initial expiration date remains December 31, 2006, while other properties have an extended date. This omission introduces ambiguity about which term applies to Ice Age II and creates a direct conflict within the document regarding its expiration date.

perturbation.original_text: The notice information for Licensee in Section 17(a) of the Agreement shall be amended such that "Paul Zuzelo" is deleted and replaced with "General Counsel", and such that the email address for Paul Zuzelo is deleted.
perturbation.changed_text: The notice information for Licensee in Section 17(a) of the Agreement shall be amended such that "Paul Zuzelo" is deleted and replaced with "General Counsel".
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and such that the email address for Paul Zuzelo is deleted', the contract now implies that Paul Zuzelo's email address should still be used for notices, even though his name has been replaced by 'General Counsel'. This creates an internal contradiction. If another section refers to 'all notices as specified in Section 17(a)', it's now unclear whether the notices should go to the General Counsel (as the name suggests) or to Paul Zuzelo's (potentially outdated) email address because it wasn't deleted. It also could be interpretated, that the 'General Counsel' is the same person as 'Paul Zuzelo', because it doesn't specify how to contact the General Counsel and only states that the name for Paul Zuzelo should be deleted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NOTICE PROVISION: The notice information for Licensee in Section 17(a) of the Agreement shall be amended such that "Paul Zuzelo" is deleted and replaced with "General Counsel".
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the deletion of Paul Zuzelo's email address, which directly contradicts the intention of updating the notice information to 'General Counsel'. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text, which now implies that notices might still go to Paul Zuzelo's email, even though his name is being replaced. This creates an ambiguity and internal contradiction about where the notices should be sent, thereby creating a strong contradiction.
